response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with solid low-latency and systems programming credentials, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application-layer software. The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, and deep neural network workload optimization — none of which appear in this resume. The closest relevant signal is a brief stint writing C/C++ components at National Instruments and low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby, but neither touches hardware architecture, silicon modeling, or ML training/inference pipelines at any level.
strengths: 14+ years of professional experience satisfies the BS+8yr education path requirement
Demonstrated low-latency systems work (Async I/O, fibers, high-performance caching at Dunnhumby) shows awareness of performance-critical software
Breadth across embedded, systems, and web domains indicates general technical adaptability
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience anywhere in the resume — the primary hard skill for this role is entirely absent
No GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator expertise; AI exposure is limited to agent-workflow tooling, not hardware or training/inference optimization
Career trajectory is firmly application/web-layer (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) with no evidence of pivoting toward hardware-software co-design or silicon-level performance analysis
